key_departures.impact: Two-time All-Pro, top-5 CB in the NFL, primary nickel/outside corner. Traded for 2026 1st (No. 29), 2026 5th, 2026 6th, 2027 3rd. Massive hole at CB1. Rams then signed him to a 4yr/$124M extension making him the highest-paid CB in NFL history.

Started 15 games in 2025. Career-high 64 tackles, 2 INTs, 6 PDs. Signed a 3yr/$51M deal with the Rams with $34M guaranteed. In a single offseason the Chiefs lost both starting CBs from their 2022 draft class.

83.5 PFF grade in 2025. Three-year starter, versatile box/deep safety. Signed 3yr/$40.25M with the Bengals. Combined with McDuffie/Watson departures, Chiefs lost 8,364 defensive snaps of the 2022 draft class secondary in one offseason.
Fourth CB from 2022 class to depart. Primarily depth/ST but had high-leverage postseason moments. Signed 2-year deal with Titans. Secondary depth now critically thin.

Stepped in when Mahomes tore his ACL in Week 14, then suffered his own season-ending knee injury. Signed 1yr/$8.25M with Arizona. Mahomes' return timeline from ACL/LCL is uncertain, leaving KC without a proven backup.
Released March 4, clearing $20M in cap space with $7.39M dead money. 53.3 PFF grade in 2025. Chronic penalty issues. Right tackle is now an open need.
Released Feb. 23, clearing ~$9M in cap space. Had been surpassed by rookie Ashton Gillotte in the rotation. Depth loss at EDGE, not a starter-level hole.
Zero 20+ yard runs in 2025. Chiefs ranked 25th in rushing yards per game (106.6). Starter-level departure but team had already identified RB as critical need. Walker signing directly replaces him.
Led team with 611 rushing yards on 163 carries (3.7 ypc). Departure is absorbed by Walker signing. At 31, was a bridge option only.
56.7 PFF grade. Slot receiver depth loss. WR corps already thin with Rice's legal situation murky and Worthy coming off shoulder surgery.
69.8 PFF grade. Another WR depth departure on a corps that was already thin. Chiefs now have Worthy, Rice (uncertain), Thornton, and Royals as primary options.
75.1 PFF grade in 2025. Part of 2022 draft class. UFA — departure status confirmed. Drue Tranquill (possible release candidate) and Cole Christiansen (re-signed) remain.

signings.analysis: Super Bowl LX MVP, highest-paid free-agent RB in NFL history. 3yr/$45M with $28.7M fully guaranteed — the richest RB free agent deal ever. Walker forced 61 missed tackles and had 10 rushes of 20+ yards in 2025. At 25, this is a true investment: $28.7M fully guaranteed on a 3-year deal signals the starter for the foreseeable future. RB drops from tier 1 critical to tier 4 — depth/late round luxury at best. The Walker signing directly addresses the Chiefs' most visible offensive failing: ranked 25th in rushing (106.6 ypg) in 2025.

Re-signed on a 1yr/$12M deal (max $15M with incentives). Kelce led the team in 2025 with 76 catches, 851 yards, 5 TDs. The one-year structure is the tell: this is his age-36 season and the Chiefs are not making a long-term TE commitment. TE remains a draft need for the future — Kelce is a bridge. Partially addresses the need for 2026, but the Chiefs should still target a TE heir in rounds 2–4 of the draft.

3yr/$24.75M with $15M guaranteed — solid mid-market starter deal. Replaces Bryan Cook (who just got $40.25M from the Bengals). Gilman was ranked 24th-best safety out of 98 qualifiers by PFF in 2025, posting 68 combined tackles, 6 PBUs, and a forced fumble. The 3-year structure with $15M guaranteed signals a real starter investment, not a bridge. However, Gilman is not a Cook-level playmaker — he partially addresses the S need. One safety spot is stabilized; a developmental or complementary S in the draft remains advisable.

3yr/$21M with $14M guaranteed. Tonga is a rare two-way interior lineman described as having traits the Chiefs haven't had since Dontari Poe — a true nose tackle who can play as an H-back/FB in short-yardage sets. Only 1.5 career sacks but an 11.4% pass-rush win rate in 2025. AAV of $7M is below the starter tier for IDL but the 3-year guaranteed structure signals the Chiefs view him as a starter. Partially addresses the IDL need — improves the run-stuffing floor, but elite pass-rush up the middle remains unresolved. Chris Jones remains the anchor.

Re-signed on 2yr/$11M (max $14M with incentives). Career highs in 2025: 438 yards (23.1 ypc avg), 3 TDs on 19 catches, plus 475 kick-return yards. Deep threat/returner with proven Mahomes chemistry. However, at $5.5M AAV this is depth-tier WR money. With Hollywood Brown and JuJu both gone, Thornton moves up the depth chart — but the Chiefs still have only Worthy, Rice (uncertain), Thornton, and Royals as WR options. WR remains a significant need. The 2-year structure does offer some commitment, but Thornton's role is still WR3/specialist, not a true WR1 or WR2 solution.
Veteran minimum re-sign. Special teams ace and LB depth. Does not address the starter-level LB need created by Chenal's departure. Need unchanged at LB.
Re-signed for a 12th season. Maintains the Butker-Winchester-Araiza special teams chemistry. Long snapper continuity secured — no impact on positional need picture.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kenneth Walker III signing (3yr/$45M, $28.7M fully guaranteed) is the highest-paid free-agent RB in NFL history. He is the clear bell-cow starter through at least 2028. RB is no longer a need. A late-round complementary back (pass-catching specialist) is possible but not a priority.

Chiefs lost three CBs in this window: McDuffie (traded, All-Pro), Watson (Rams, 3yr/$51M), and Joshua Williams (Titans). Nohl Williams (rookie who started in 2025) and Kristian Fulton are the primary options left. With the No. 9 overall pick and a desperate secondary, CB is now the team's top draft priority. Critical tier 1 need.

Jawaan Taylor was released March 4, clearing $20M but leaving the right tackle spot completely open. No replacement has been signed. With Mahomes recovering from ACL surgery and expected to return in 2026, protecting him is paramount. OT is a tier 1 need — must be addressed in the draft or remaining free agency.

Gardner Minshew departed for Arizona (1yr/$8.25M). Mahomes' ACL/LCL recovery timeline is uncertain. Chris Oladokun is the only QB on roster. The backup QB market has thinned significantly (Mariota, Huntley, Minshew all gone). KC needs a credible bridge/backup — Geno Smith and Kirk Cousins are the reported targets. Until a proven backup is signed, QB is a tier 2 need.

Thornton re-signing (2yr/$11M) provides minimal relief. Hollywood Brown and JuJu Smith-Schuster are both gone. Rashee Rice's situation is uncertain (legal issues, contract year). The corps is: Worthy (signed through 2027, injury concerns), Rice (uncertain), Thornton (depth-tier), Royals (raw rookie). WR remains a significant need — should target in rounds 1–3 of the draft or remaining free agency.

Bryan Cook's departure (3yr/$40.25M to Bengals) created a new starter-level need. Alohi Gilman's signing (3yr/$24.75M, $15M guaranteed) partially fills the hole with a legitimate starter. However, Gilman is not a Cook-level playmaker. Safety drops from tier 1 emergency to tier 3 — a developmental or complementary safety in rounds 3–5 of the draft makes sense.

Khyiris Tonga (3yr/$21M, $14M guaranteed) improves the run-stuffing floor alongside Chris Jones. Tonga brings rare two-way capability at nose tackle. However, Jones is still the anchor and aging — IDL depth/pass-rush from the interior remains a draft consideration in rounds 2–4.

Kelce re-signed (1yr/$12M) stabilizes 2026, but the one-year bridge structure means the heir-apparent search continues. Robert Tonyan Jr. is also a free agent. Chiefs should still look for a TE of the future in rounds 2–4 of the draft. Need level unchanged — still a genuine draft target.

Mike Danna was released (rotational loss, not a starter-level departure). George Karlaftis and Ashton Gillotte remain. No EDGE was added in free agency. EDGE remains a tier 2 need — should target a starter-caliber rusher in the draft or late free agency.

Leo Chenal (75.1 PFF) departed as a UFA. Cole Christiansen was re-signed at a minimum deal. Drue Tranquill is a possible release candidate. LB depth is eroding — escalates to tier 2. The draft should address this in rounds 2–4.

key_additions.headline: Super Bowl LX MVP and highest-paid free-agent RB ever ends KC's backfield misery; directly replaces Pacheco/Hunt with an elite explosive threat
Three-year starter replaces departed Bryan Cook; PFF top-25 safety brings range and tackling reliability to a gutted KC secondary
Rare two-way nose tackle raises IDL floor alongside Chris Jones; first Dontari Poe-type interior weapon Andy Reid has had in years
Legend returns for 14th season on bridge deal; led team with 76 catches and 851 yards in 2025, stabilizes Mahomes' top target through the ACL recovery window
Deep threat/returner re-signs after 23.1 ypc average in 2025; retains key Mahomes connection as WR corps rebuild begins

key_losses.headline: Two-time All-Pro and highest-paid CB in NFL history departs via blockbuster trade; KC gets two first-round picks in 2026 but loses their defensive cornerstone
Chiefs lose both starting CBs from the 2022 draft class in one offseason, creating a critical secondary crisis heading into the draft
Versatile 83.5-PFF-grade safety departs for Cincinnati; combined with McDuffie and Watson losses, the Chiefs' 2022 secondary class is almost entirely gone
Right tackle released for cap relief, opening a critical OT need with Mahomes returning from ACL surgery
Backup QB who started after Mahomes' ACL tear departs for a starting opportunity in Arizona, leaving KC without a proven backup behind a recovering Mahomes
